page: Amazon Web Services r6a.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core testing placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance is weaker, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark database operations scoring in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks also fall into the bottom 25% category. Single-threaded tasks, such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, perform at average levels, as does the overall Passmark CPU and memory score.

Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8:1 and strong single-core performance, the r6a.xlarge is suited for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high parallel processing power or high local storage throughput. Appropriate workloads include medium-sized in-memory caches, lightweight database nodes, and single-threaded data processing tasks. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, and the 2 Gbps network baseline limits high-throughput data transfer.
description: Amazon Web Services r6a.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory, delivering an 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor, it offers strong single-core CPU performance, scoring in the top 25% in stress-ng single-core benchmarks. However, multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the bottom 25%. The instance lacks local storage and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy applications that rely on single-threaded execution rather than massive parallelization. Best-fit workloads include in-memory caching, text processing, and lightweight database deployments.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6a.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor, delivering strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6a.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 3.6 GHz clock speed
32.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing an 8:1 memory-to-core ratio
Strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
No local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Suited for in-memory caching, text processing, and lightweight databases
